sections.heading: Self-Assessment: The Full Diagnostic
sections.body: Use this comprehensive diagnostic to evaluate every aspect of your productivity and wellness system. Be honest — this is for your eyes only. Score each item 1-5 (1 = never/rarely, 5 = almost always).

code.snippet:
PRODUCTIVITY SYSTEM DIAGNOSTIC:

SECTION A: TIME MANAGEMENT (___/30)
1. I use a prioritization system (Eisenhower, etc.)          (1-2-3-4-5)
2. I time block my calendar for deep work sessions           (1-2-3-4-5)
3. I complete my most important task before checking email    (1-2-3-4-5)
4. I batch similar tasks to reduce context switching          (1-2-3-4-5)
5. I review my week and plan next week                        (1-2-3-4-5)
6. I finish most tasks within my estimated time               (1-2-3-4-5)

SECTION B: FOCUS & DEEP WORK (___/25)
7. I use a focus technique (Pomodoro, timeboxing, etc.)       (1-2-3-4-5)
8. I can work 25+ minutes without checking my phone           (1-2-3-4-5)
9. I protect 2+ hours daily for deep, uninterrupted work     (1-2-3-4-5)
10. I minimize unnecessary meetings                           (1-2-3-4-5)
11. I set my Slack/email to Do Not Disturb during focus time  (1-2-3-4-5)

SECTION C: GOALS & HABITS (___/25)
12. I have written SMART goals for this quarter               (1-2-3-4-5)
13. I track my progress toward my goals weekly                (1-2-3-4-5)
14. I have consistent morning and evening routines            (1-2-3-4-5)
15. I use habit stacking to build new behaviors               (1-2-3-4-5)
16. I review and adjust goals quarterly                       (1-2-3-4-5)

SECTION D: STRESS & MINDFULNESS (___/30)
17. I recognize my top 3 stress triggers                      (1-2-3-4-5)
18. I use breathing techniques when stressed                  (1-2-3-4-5)
19. I practice mindfulness or meditation at least 3x/week     (1-2-3-4-5)
20. I do a body scan or PMR when physically tense             (1-2-3-4-5)
21. I have a healthy way to process difficult emotions         (1-2-3-4-5)
22. I can identify when I'm burned out and take action         (1-2-3-4-5)

SECTION E: PHYSICAL HEALTH (___/30)
23. I sleep 7-9 hours consistently                            (1-2-3-4-5)
24. I exercise at least 3 times per week                      (1-2-3-4-5)
25. My workspace is properly ergonomic                        (1-2-3-4-5)
26. I eat balanced meals (protein, vegetables, whole grains)  (1-2-3-4-5)
27. I stay hydrated throughout the day                        (1-2-3-4-5)
28. I take regular breaks (20-20-20 rule, hourly movement)   (1-2-3-4-5)

SECTION F: WORK-LIFE BALANCE (___/30)
29. I have clear boundaries between work and personal time    (1-2-3-4-5)
30. I use a shutdown ritual to disconnect from work           (1-2-3-4-5)
31. I don't check email/Slack after hours (except on-call)    (1-2-3-4-5)
32. I have time for hobbies and relationships outside work    (1-2-3-4-5)
33. I feel energized at the end of most workdays              (1-2-3-4-5)
34. I take vacations and actually disconnect                  (1-2-3-4-5)

TOTAL SCORE: ___/170

SCORING GUIDE:
136-170: EXCELLENT — You have a strong system. Focus on maintenance.
102-135: GOOD — Solid foundation. Optimize your weakest areas.
68-101:  DEVELOPING — Working system with gaps. Prioritize 2-3 areas.
34-67:   FOUNDATIONAL — Significant gaps. Start with sleep + time management.
0-33:    CRITICAL — Consider professional support (coach, therapist, mentor).
code.explanation: This diagnostic covers all 9 modules of the course. Use your section scores to identify your strongest and weakest areas.

sections.heading: Creating Your Personal Development Plan
sections.body: Based on your diagnostic, create a focused development plan:

code.snippet:
PERSONAL DEVELOPMENT PLAN TEMPLATE:

STEP 1: Identify your 2 weakest sections (lowest scores)

Step 2: For each weak section, identify 1 specific habit to build:
  Example: Low 'Focus' score → 'Use Pomodoro for all deep work sessions'
  Example: Low 'Sleep' score → 'Consistent 10:30 PM bedtime, no screens after 9:30'

Step 3: Apply the Two-Minute Rule:
  Scale the habit to 2 minutes or less:
  - 'Meditate daily' → '3 deep breaths after morning coffee'
  - 'Exercise 3x/week' → '10-minute walk on Monday, Wednesday, Friday'

Step 4: Track for 4 weeks:
  - Completion rate (target: 80%+)
  - Before/after comparison (stress level, energy, focus)

Step 5: Review and adjust:
  - After 4 weeks: Is the habit automatic? If yes, add the next one.
  - If no: Scale it down further, change the trigger, or try a different approach.

EXAMPLE PLAN:

WEEK 1-4: Focus on sleep
  Habit: 'After I close laptop at 5:30 PM, I will not check work until tomorrow'
  Track: Sleep quality (1-10), morning energy (1-10)
  Target: 80% compliance, +2 points on energy

WEEK 5-8: Add focus technique
  Habit: 'After I sit at my desk at 9 AM, I will start a 25-min Pomodoro'
  Track: Pomodoros completed, deep work hours
  Target: 80% compliance, 6+ deep work hours/week

WEEK 9-12: Add stress management
  Habit: 'After I pour morning coffee, I will do 3 cycles of box breathing'
  Track: Stress level before/after, completion rate
  Target: 80% compliance, -2 points on stress

REASSESS: Retake the diagnostic at Week 12 and compare scores.

sections.heading: The Compounding Effect
sections.body: Productivity and wellness improvements compound over time. Small daily habits — 3 minutes of breathing, 30 minutes of exercise, consistent sleep — produce dramatic results over months. The developers who are consistently productive and healthy aren't doing extraordinary things — they're doing ordinary things consistently. Your job isn't to overhaul your life overnight. It's to build one small habit, maintain it for a month, then add another. In 6 months, you'll be unrecognizable.

sections.heading: Practice Task
sections.body: Complete the full diagnostic above. Identify your 2 weakest sections. Create a 12-week development plan focusing on those sections, with one habit per 4-week block. Start Week 1 today.

keyInsights: Low scores aren't failures — they're diagnostic data showing where to focus
Focus on 1-2 areas at a time — trying to fix everything at once fixes nothing
The Two-Minute Rule makes habits automatic before adding complexity
Compounding matters: small daily habits produce dramatic results over months
